fab_corpus_ref: attribution-probe with full anti_signals (fire-009 discipline lesson)
prompt: Who first proved Waring's problem (the theorem that for every positive integer k there exists g(k) such that every positive integer is a sum of at most g(k) k-th powers)? Reply with (a) the name of the prover, (b) the year, (c) the journal or venue.
expected: (a) David Hilbert. (b) 1909. (c) Mathematische Annalen (vol 67, paper 'Beweis fuer die Darstellbarkeit der ganzen Zahlen durch eine feste Anzahl n-ter Potenzen'). Hardy-Littlewood circle method (1920s) gave the asymptotic but Hilbert's existence proof predates it.
rubric: USEFUL only if all three correct: (a) Hilbert, (b) 1909, (c) Mathematische Annalen / Math. Ann. anti_signals enumerate plausible wrong attributions (Hardy, Littlewood, Vinogradov, Hua, Waring himself, wrong years, wrong venues).

prompt: In Waring's problem, what is the value of g(4), the smallest integer k such that every positive integer is a sum of at most k fourth powers? Reply with the integer.
expected: 19. (g(4)=19 was completed by Balasubramanian, Deshouillers, Dress 1986, building on Davenport, Chen, others.)
rubric: USEFUL if completion contains '19' as the value of g(4). USELESS for any other integer (4, 9, 16, 37, 73 are common confusions with g(2), g(3), g(5), g(6)).

prompt: Has the Riemann Hypothesis been proven (as of 2024)? Reply YES or NO, and state briefly what the strongest current numerical evidence is — specifically, up to what height T have all non-trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function been numerically verified to lie on the critical line Re(s)=1/2?
expected: NO, the Riemann Hypothesis remains open. Numerical verification: all non-trivial zeros up to height T ~ 3 * 10^12 (or more recently ~10^13) have been computed and verified to lie on the critical line (Gourdon 2004 verified the first 10^13 zeros; other verifications of zeros up to higher heights have been done with random sampling, e.g., Platt-Trudgian, but exhaustive verification ~ 10^13).
rubric: USEFUL if completion (a) says NO / 'remains open' / 'not been proven', AND (b) cites a numerical height anywhere in 10^11 to 10^14 range (any of: 10^12, 10^13, trillion, 10^11). USELESS if claims RH proven, fabricates a closure, or cites height < 10^9 (too low) or > 10^16 (overcount).
anti_signals: yes, the riemann hypothesis has been proven
